name: Kursk Oblast (Russia)

colors.symbolism: the outer stripes; recalls the flag of the Soviet Union
silver stripe of the Romanov tricolour
gold stripe of the Romanov tricolour
black stripe of the Romanov tricolour

description: Five horizontal stripes - red, white, yellow, black, red - in proportions 2:1:1:1:2, with the Kursk Oblast coat of arms across the central three narrow stripes.
history: Adopted under regional law N19-3KO. Combines the historic black-yellow-white tricolour of the Russian Empire (the Romanov flag) with the red of the Soviet flag.

features.symbolism: combines the black-yellow-white tricolour of the Romanov-era Russian Empire flag with the red of the Soviet Union flag.
the oblast coat of arms charged across the central white, yellow, and black stripes.
